context: AP Inter (Class XI–XII) under BIE-AP, NCERT-aligned syllabus introduced 2025–26. Most states follow similar patterns under their boards.

streams.name: MPC
streams.description: The most-chosen stream in AP and South India. Default route to engineering, computer science, IT, and architecture — but also opens commerce careers (CA, CS) and competitive exams that don't restrict by stream (UPSC, CLAT, NDA, IPMAT).
best_for: students who enjoy problem-solving
students aiming at engineering, IT, architecture, defence (Air Force/Navy via NDA), or actuarial science
students who haven't yet decided — MPC keeps the most doors open
not_for: students who dislike maths or physics — they will struggle with 12th board and entrance prep
students who clearly want medicine — BiPC is required for NEET
students who are sure about commerce — MEC/CEC is more aligned
alumni_typical_paths: B.Tech + IPMAT or CAT → consulting (₹25–50 LPA)
streams.source: BIE Andhra Pradesh, NCERT, India Skills Report 2026

streams.name: BiPC
streams.description: The medical stream. Required for NEET-UG (MBBS, BDS, AYUSH, B.Sc Nursing, B.Pharm). Also opens biotech, agriculture, veterinary, biomedical research, and forensic science. Cannot do JEE without Maths — that closes engineering.
best_for: students aiming at medicine (MBBS, BDS, AYUSH, Pharmacy)
students interested in biology, life sciences, biotech, agriculture, environmental science
students wanting nursing, allied health (physiotherapy, occupational therapy, audiology)
not_for: students wanting engineering — JEE needs Maths
students wanting CA via the standard route — possible but harder without Maths
alumni_typical_paths: MBBS → MD/MS → super-specialist (₹25 LPA–1Cr+ at 10 yr)
B.Pharm → MBA Pharma → Big Pharma (₹15–30 LPA)
B.Sc Nursing → AIIMS/Apollo → ₹6–15 LPA, M.Sc Nursing path
streams.source: BIE Andhra Pradesh, NMC India, NCERT

streams.name: MBiPC
streams.description: Introduced in AP from 2025–26. A 6-subject combined group letting MPC students take Botany or BiPC students take Maths to keep BOTH JEE and NEET routes open. Heavier workload but maximum optionality.
best_for: genuinely undecided students between engineering and medicine
students who are strong academically and can handle the extra subject
students interested in biomedical engineering, biotech engineering, biostatistics — fields requiring both
not_for: weaker students — the 6-subject load is real
students with a clear stream preference — pick MPC or BiPC and go deep
streams.source: BIE Andhra Pradesh 2025–26 reform, Sakshi Education

streams.name: MEC
streams.description: The commerce-with-maths stream. Strong base for CA, CMA, CS, CFA, B.Com (H), economics honours, IPMAT, statistics, actuarial science. Maths gives an edge for finance careers and economics at Ashoka/DU.
best_for: students aiming at CA, CS, CMA, CFA, banking, investment banking
students who want B.Com (H), B.A. Economics (H), Statistics, BBA Finance
IPMAT or JIPMAT aspirants — quant section is heavy
not_for: students who dislike maths — go CEC instead
students aiming at engineering or medicine
streams.source: BIE Andhra Pradesh, India Skills Report 2026

streams.name: CEC
streams.description: Commerce without maths. Direct route to CA, CS, CMA via Foundation, plus law (CLAT/AILET), civil services, journalism, and public policy. Makes IPMAT harder (no Maths in XII), but everything else is open.
best_for: students aiming at CA/CS/CMA who don't need Maths
law aspirants (CLAT, AILET, SLAT)
civil services, journalism, mass communication, social work aspirants
not_for: students wanting IPMAT or BBA finance — Maths is needed

streams.name: HEC / Humanities
streams.description: Pure humanities. Best for students drawn to history, sociology, political science, languages, philosophy, literature, archaeology, journalism, and policy work. Liberal Arts undergrad at Ashoka, Krea, FLAME, Azim Premji, plus DU/JNU/BHU via CUET.
best_for: students drawn to ideas, writing, debate, social systems
future lawyers, journalists, civil servants, academics, policy researchers
students who want a liberal-arts undergraduate path
not_for: students looking for the most direct vocational pipeline
streams.source: BIE Andhra Pradesh, India Skills Report 2026

streams.name: Vocational / ITI / Polytechnic
streams.full_name: Industrial Training Institute and Polytechnic Diploma
subjects: trade-specific (electrician, fitter, welder, COPA, mechanic, etc.)
streams.description: Skill-based, ready-to-earn pathway. ITI is 6 months–2 years; Polytechnic is 3 years. Direct entry to government/PSU jobs (Railways, ONGC, BHEL), private industry, and self-employment. Polytechnic also allows lateral entry to 2nd year B.Tech.
best_for: students who want to start earning quickly
students who learn by doing rather than theory
families that need income within 2–3 years
not_for: students aiming at competitive professional careers (medicine/law) — wrong starting point
streams.source: DGT 2026 reforms, India Skills Report 2026 (ITI 45.95% employability, polytechnic 32.92%)
